title: First stranger engagement on launch content: dev.to commenter Mehmet Can Farsak (Brainstorm-Mode)
summary: 2026 06 13: first real outside engagement on the launch dev.to article 'prove itself' . Mehmet Can Farsak GitHub: mehmetcanfarsak commented + liked: praised the 'prove it / receipts for memory' framing, plugged his own B
body: 2026-06-13: first real outside engagement on the launch dev.to article ('prove itself'). Mehmet Can Farsak (GitHub: mehmetcanfarsak) commented + liked: praised the 'prove it / receipts for memory' framing, plugged his own Brainstorm-Mode (PreToolUse-hook tool enforcing agent mode: divergent/actionable/academic). Replied as founder in peer-builder voice ('same disease, different organs' — his verifies agent MODE before acting, ours verifies the MEMORY it acts on; both ride PreToolUse hooks), said I'd try his tool, no sell. He's exactly our audience (builds agent-hook tooling, active on dev.to) and a potential amplifier/cross-promo. Follow-up: actually look at Brainstorm-Mode; consider a genuine mutual mention if it's good. Lesson: technical-peer comments want peer reciprocity, not a pitch.
